rationale: `src/types.ts` declares the canonical `interface Coin { id, symbol, name, price, change_24h }`. `src/components/CoinRow.tsx` REDECLARES `interface Coin { name, value }` locally — a totally different shape. tsc TS2300 would catch this ONLY if both decls were in the same scope; here they're in different files (different scopes), so tsc lets it through. The consumer uses the partial local Coin, the API delivers the full canonical Coin, and the symbol / price / change_24h fields are silently dropped — the user sees blank cells where prices should be. This is the Crypto-Dashboard rework bug: two agents wrote two definitions for the same conceptual type, neither cross-referenced the other. `duplicate_type_declarations` walks every interface/type declaration, groups by name, and flags any name with >=2 distinct member-sets across different files (TS interface merging is excluded — same shape across files is fine).

baseline_comparison.tsc: PASS — TS2300 only fires for same-scope duplicates. Different files, different scopes, both compile.
baseline_comparison.pylint_jsequiv: N/A — this is a TS-specific failure mode.
baseline_comparison.eslint: PARTIAL — there are some plugins that look for type duplication, but none catch the SHAPE divergence; they only catch literal name reuse.
baseline_comparison.raw_llm_critique: INCONSISTENT — sometimes spots the redeclaration, often reads as 'careful local typing'.
baseline_comparison.manual_QA: Catches when the user sees blank cells — but it's silent on first review and easy to miss in code review.
